tax: Placed at family rank, Worked-Earth Family is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a mixed-chemistry earth body from a contact seam or organic channel. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a mixed-earth body with both mineral and organic components.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a pale crumbling figure or a dark slumping mound. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — gritty pale crumbles or a dark humic smear — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Worked-Earth Family occupies marl flats, clay-limestone contacts, and root channels. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where earth-pressure animates a mixed-chemistry accumulation and recur with the contact or channel replenishes, sustaining themselves on earth-pressure in mixed chemistry.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: entombs or adheres with its bulk. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: mixed-earth body and seam-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: entombing and adhesion. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Worked-Earth Family matters because makes marl flats and organic channels entombment traps. It lets survey reports sort mixed-earth hazards by chemistry — crumbling pale mass versus dark adhesion without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Worked-Earth Family is the family-rank grouping of mixed organic-mineral earth elementals of marl and loam within the True Elementals. Its members share a body of earth containing both mineral and organic chemistry, and divide below into genus groups by lime-dominant pale marl versus organics-dominant dark loam. The worked-earth family haunts the softer soils of the upper tiers.
